heading: Validity and priority against certain persons
label: 6323 Validity and priority against certain persons
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title

Effective Date of 1966 Amendments–(c), , , 1147, provided that:Pub. L. 89–719, title I, § 114(a)80 Stat. 1146Nov. 2, 1966“(a).—General RuleExcept as otherwise provided, the amendments made by this title [enacting sections 3505, 7425, 7426, and 7810 of this title, amending this section, sections 545, 6322, 6324, 6325, 6331, 6332, 6334, 6335, 6337, 6338, 6339, 6342, 6343, 6502, 6503, 6532, 7402, 7403, 7421, 7424, 7505, 7506, and 7809 of this title, and section 270a of former Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, redesignating former section 7425 as 7427 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and ] shall apply after the date of enactment of this Act [], regardless of when a lien or a title of the United States arose or when the lien or interest of any other person was acquired.section 7424 of this titleNov. 2, 1966“(b).—ExceptionsThe amendments made by this title shall not apply in any case—“(1)in which a lien or a title derived from enforcement of a lien held by the United States has been enforced by a civil action or suit which has become final by judgment, sale, or agreement before the date of enactment of this Act; or“(2)in which such amendments would—“(A)impair a priority enjoyed by any person (other than the United States) holding a lien or interest prior to the date of enactment of this Act;“(B)operate to increase the liability of any such person; or“(C)shorten the time for bringing suit with respect to transactions occurring before the date of enactment of this Act.“(c)Liability for Withheld Taxes.—“(1)The amendments made by section 105(a) (relating to effect on third parties) [adding ] shall apply only with respect to wages paid on or after .section 3505 of this titleJanuary 1, 1967“(2)The amendments made by section 105(b) (relating to performance bonds of contractors for public buildings or works) [amending section 270a of former Title 40] shall apply to contracts entered into pursuant to invitations for bids issued after .”June 30, 1967, , , provided that:Pub. L. 89–493, § 2180 Stat. 266July 5, 1966“This Act [amending this section] shall take effect on the first day of the first month which is at least ninety days after the date of approval of this Act [].”July 5, 1966

children.heading: Purchasers, holders of security interests, mechanic’s lienors, and judgment lien creditors
children.label: a Purchasers, holders of security interests, mechanic’s lienors, and judgment lien creditors
children.text: The lien imposed by section 6321 shall not be valid as against any purchaser, holder of a security interest, mechanic’s lienor, or judgment lien creditor until notice thereof which meets the requirements of subsection (f) has been filed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Protection for certain interests even though notice filed
children.label: b Protection for certain interests even though notice filed
A
children.text: as against a purchaser of such security who at the time of purchase did not have actual notice or knowledge of the existence of such lien; and

children.label: B
children.text: as against a holder of a security interest in such security who, at the time such interest came into existence, did not have actual notice or knowledge of the existence of such lien.

children.label: A
children.text: at the time of the purchase such purchaser did not have actual notice or knowledge of the existence of such lien, and

children.label: B
children.text: before the purchaser obtains such notice or knowledge, he has acquired possession of such motor vehicle and has not thereafter relinquished possession of such motor vehicle to the seller or his agent.

children.heading: Personal property purchased at retail
children.label: 3 Personal property purchased at retail
children.text: With respect to tangible personal property purchased at retail, as against a purchaser in the ordinary course of the seller’s trade or business, unless at the time of such purchase such purchaser intends such purchase to (or knows such purchase will) hinder, evade, or defeat the collection of any tax under this title.

children.heading: Personal property purchased in casual sale
children.label: 4 Personal property purchased in casual sale
children.text: With respect to household goods, personal effects, or other tangible personal property described in section 6334(a) purchased (not for resale) in a casual sale for less than $1,000, as against the purchaser, but only if such purchaser does not have actual notice or knowledge (A) of the existence of such lien, or (B) that this sale is one of a series of sales.

children.heading: Personal property subject to possessory lien
children.label: 5 Personal property subject to possessory lien
children.text: With respect to tangible personal property subject to a lien under local law securing the reasonable price of the repair or improvement of such property, as against a holder of such a lien, if such holder is, and has been, continuously in possession of such property from the time such lien arose.

children.heading: Real property tax and special assessment liens
children.label: 6 Real property tax and special assessment liens
A
children.text: a tax of general application levied by any taxing authority based upon the value of such property;

children.label: B
children.text: a special assessment imposed directly upon such property by any taxing authority, if such assessment is imposed for the purpose of defraying the cost of any public improvement; or

children.label: C
children.text: charges for utilities or public services furnished to such property by the United States, a State or political subdivision thereof, or an instrumentality of any one or more of the foregoing.

children.heading: Residential property subject to a mechanic’s lien for certain repairs and improvements
children.label: 7 Residential property subject to a mechanic’s lien for certain repairs and improvements
children.text: With respect to real property subject to a lien for repair or improvement of a personal residence (containing not more than four dwelling units) occupied by the owner of such residence, as against a mechanic’s lienor, but only if the contract price on the contract with the owner is not more than $5,000.

children.heading: Attorneys’ liens
children.label: 8 Attorneys’ liens
children.text: With respect to a judgment or other amount in settlement of a claim or of a cause of action, as against an attorney who, under local law, holds a lien upon or a contract enforcible against such judgment or amount, to the extent of his reasonable compensation for obtaining such judgment or procuring such settlement, except that this paragraph shall not apply to any judgment or amount in settlement of a claim or of a cause of action against the United States to the extent that the United States offsets such judgment or amount against any liability of the taxpayer to the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: before such organization had actual notice or knowledge of the existence of such lien;

children.label: B
children.text: after such organization had such notice or knowledge, with respect to advances required to be made automatically to maintain such contract in force under an agreement entered into before such organization had such notice or knowledge; or

children.label: C
children.text: after satisfaction of a levy pursuant to section 6332(b), unless and until the Secretary delivers to such organization a notice, executed after the date of such satisfaction, of the existence of such lien.

children.heading: Deposit-secured loans
children.label: 10 Deposit-secured loans
children.text: With respect to a savings deposit, share, or other account with an institution described in section 581 or 591, to the extent of any loan made by such institution without actual notice or knowledge of the existence of such lien, as against such institution, if such loan is secured by such account.

children.heading: Protection for certain commercial transactions financing agreements, etc.
children.label: c Protection for certain commercial transactions financing agreements, etc.
i
children.text: a commercial transactions financing agreement,

children.label: ii
children.text: a real property construction or improvement financing agreement, or

children.label: iii
children.text: an obligatory disbursement agreement, and

children.label: B
children.text: is protected under local law against a judgment lien arising, as of the time of tax lien filing, out of an unsecured obligation.

children.label: i
children.text: to make loans to the taxpayer to be secured by commercial financing security acquired by the taxpayer in the ordinary course of his trade or business, or

children.label: ii
children.text: to purchase commercial financing security (other than inventory) acquired by the taxpayer in the ordinary course of his trade or business;

children.heading: Limitation on qualified property
children.label: B Limitation on qualified property
children.text: The term “qualified property”, when used with respect to a commercial transactions financing agreement, includes only commercial financing security acquired by the taxpayer before the 46th day after the date of tax lien filing.

children.heading: Commercial financing security defined
children.label: C Commercial financing security defined
children.text: The term “commercial financing security” means (i) paper of a kind ordinarily arising in commercial transactions, (ii) accounts receivable, (iii) mortgages on real property, and (iv) inventory.

children.heading: Purchaser treated as acquiring security interest
children.label: D Purchaser treated as acquiring security interest
children.text: A person who satisfies subparagraph (A) by reason of clause (ii) thereof shall be treated as having acquired a security interest in commercial financing security

children.heading: Real property construction or improvement financing agreement
children.label: 3 Real property construction or improvement financing agreement
i
children.text: the construction or improvement of real property,

children.label: ii
children.text: a contract to construct or improve real property, or

children.label: iii
children.text: the raising or harvesting of a farm crop or the raising of livestock or other animals.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of subparagraph (A)(i), the real property with respect to which the construction or improvement has been or is to be made,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of subparagraph (A)(ii), the proceeds of the contract described therein, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of subparagraph (A)(iii), property subject to the lien imposed by section 6321 at the time of tax lien filing and the crop or the livestock or other animals referred to in subparagraph (A)(iii).

children.heading: Definition
children.label: A Definition
children.text: The term “obligatory disbursement agreement” means an agreement (entered into by a person in the course of his trade or business) to make disbursements, but such an agreement shall be treated as coming within the term only to the extent of disbursements which are required to be made by reason of the intervention of the rights of a person other than the taxpayer.

children.heading: Limitation on qualified property
children.label: B Limitation on qualified property
children.text: The term “qualified property”, when used with respect to an obligatory disbursement agreement, means property subject to the lien imposed by section 6321 at the time of tax lien filing and (to the extent that the acquisition is directly traceable to the disbursements referred to in subparagraph (A)) property acquired by the taxpayer after tax lien filing.

children.heading: Special rules for surety agreements
children.label: C Special rules for surety agreements
i
children.text: the term “qualified property” shall be treated as also including the proceeds of the contract the performance of which was ensured, and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the contract the performance of which was ensured was a contract to construct or improve real property, to produce goods, or to furnish services, the term “qualified property” shall be treated as also including any tangible personal property used by the taxpayer in the performance of such ensured contract.
is in property (A) subject, at the time of tax lien filing, to the lien imposed by section 6321, and (B) covered by the terms of a written agreement entered into before tax lien filing, and
is protected under local law against a judgment lien arising, as of the time of tax lien filing, out of an unsecured obligation.

children.heading: Priority of interest and expenses
children.label: e Priority of interest and expenses

children.text: any interest or carrying charges upon the obligation secured,
the reasonable charges and expenses of an indenture trustee or agent holding the security interest for the benefit of the holder of the security interest,
the reasonable expenses, including reasonable compensation for attorneys, actually incurred in collecting or enforcing the obligation secured,
the reasonable costs of insuring, preserving, or repairing the property to which the lien or security interest relates,
the reasonable costs of insuring payment of the obligation secured, and
amounts paid to satisfy any lien on the property to which the lien or security interest relates, but only if the lien so satisfied is entitled to priority over the lien imposed by section 6321,

children.heading: Place for filing notice; form
children.label: f Place for filing notice; form

children.heading: Real property
children.label: i Real property
children.text: In the case of real property, in one office within the State (or the county, or other governmental subdivision), as designated by the laws of such State, in which the property subject to the lien is situated; and

children.heading: Personal property
children.label: ii Personal property
children.text: In the case of personal property, whether tangible or intangible, in one office within the State (or the county, or other governmental subdivision), as designated by the laws of such State, in which the property subject to the lien is situated, except that State law merely conforming to or reenacting Federal law establishing a national filing system does not constitute a second office for filing as designated by the laws of such State; or

children.heading: With clerk of district court
children.label: B With clerk of district court
children.text: In the office of the clerk of the United States district court for the judicial district in which the property subject to the lien is situated, whenever the State has not by law designated one office which meets the requirements of subparagraph (A); or

children.heading: With Recorder of Deeds of the District of Columbia
children.label: C With Recorder of Deeds of the District of Columbia
children.text: In the office of the Recorder of Deeds of the District of Columbia, if the property subject to the lien is situated in the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Situs of property subject to lien
children.label: 2 Situs of property subject to lien

children.heading: Real property
children.label: A Real property
children.text: In the case of real property, at its physical location; or

children.heading: Personal property
children.label: B Personal property
children.text: In the case of personal property, whether tangible or intangible, at the residence of the taxpayer at the time the notice of lien is filed.

children.heading: Form
children.label: 3 Form
children.text: The form and content of the notice referred to in subsection (a) shall be prescribed by the Secretary. Such notice shall be valid notwithstanding any other provision of law regarding the form or content of a notice of lien.

children.heading: Indexing required with respect to certain real property
children.label: 4 Indexing required with respect to certain real property
A
children.text: under the laws of the State in which the real property is located, a deed is not valid as against a purchaser of the property who (at the time of purchase) does not have actual notice or knowledge of the existence of such deed unless the fact of filing of such deed has been entered and recorded in a public index at the place of filing in such a manner that a reasonable inspection of the index will reveal the existence of the deed, and

children.label: B
children.text: there is maintained (at the applicable office under paragraph (1)) an adequate system for the public indexing of Federal tax liens,

children.heading: National filing systems
children.label: 5 National filing systems
children.text: The filing of a notice of lien shall be governed solely by this title and shall not be subject to any other Federal law establishing a place or places for the filing of liens or encumbrances under a national filing system.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Unless notice of lien is refiled in the manner prescribed in paragraph (2) during the required refiling period, such notice of lien shall be treated as filed on the date on which it is filed (in accordance with subsection (f)) after the expiration of such refiling period.

children.label: i
children.text: such notice of lien is refiled in the office in which the prior notice of lien was filed, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of real property, the fact of refiling is entered and recorded in an index to the extent required by subsection (f)(4); and

children.label: B
children.text: in any case in which, 90 days or more prior to the date of a refiling of notice of lien under subparagraph (A), the Secretary received written information (in the manner prescribed in regulations issued by the Secretary) concerning a change in the taxpayer’s residence, if a notice of such lien is also filed in accordance with subsection (f) in the State in which such residence is located.

children.label: A
children.text: the one-year period ending 30 days after the expiration of 10 years after the date of the assessment of the tax, and

children.label: B
children.text: the one-year period ending with the expiration of 10 years after the close of the preceding required refiling period for such notice of lien.

children.heading: Transitional rule
children.label: 4 Transitional rule
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (3), if the assessment of the tax was made before , the first required refiling period shall be the calendar year 1967.January 1, 1962

children.heading: Security interest
children.label: 1 Security interest
children.text: The term “security interest” means any interest in property acquired by contract for the purpose of securing payment or performance of an obligation or indemnifying against loss or liability. A security interest exists at any time (A) if, at such time, the property is in existence and the interest has become protected under local law against a subsequent judgment lien arising out of an unsecured obligation, and (B) to the extent that, at such time, the holder has parted with money or money’s worth.

children.heading: Mechanic’s lienor
children.label: 2 Mechanic’s lienor
children.text: The term “mechanic’s lienor” means any person who under local law has a lien on real property (or on the proceeds of a contract relating to real property) for services, labor, or materials furnished in connection with the construction or improvement of such property. For purposes of the preceding sentence, a person has a lien on the earliest date such lien becomes valid under local law against subsequent purchasers without actual notice, but not before he begins to furnish the services, labor, or materials.

children.heading: Motor vehicle
children.label: 3 Motor vehicle
children.text: The term “motor vehicle” means a self-propelled vehicle which is registered for highway use under the laws of any State or foreign country.

children.heading: Security
children.label: 4 Security
children.text: The term “security” means any bond, debenture, note, or certificate or other evidence of indebtedness, issued by a corporation or a government or political subdivision thereof, with interest coupons or in registered form, share of stock, voting trust certificate, or any certificate of interest or participation in, certificate of deposit or receipt for, temporary or interim certificate for, or warrant or right to subscribe to or purchase, any of the foregoing; negotiable instrument; or money.

children.heading: Tax lien filing
children.label: 5 Tax lien filing
children.text: The term “tax lien filing” means the filing of notice (referred to in subsection (a)) of the lien imposed by section 6321.

children.label: A
children.text: a lease of property,

children.label: B
children.text: a written executory contract to purchase or lease property,

children.label: C
children.text: an option to purchase or lease property or any interest therein, or

children.label: D
children.text: an option to renew or extend a lease of property,

children.heading: Actual notice or knowledge
children.label: 1 Actual notice or knowledge
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, an organization shall be deemed for purposes of a particular transaction to have actual notice or knowledge of any fact from the time such fact is brought to the attention of the individual conducting such transaction, and in any event from the time such fact would have been brought to such individual’s attention if the organization had exercised due diligence. An organization exercises due diligence if it maintains reasonable routines for communicating significant information to the person conducting the transaction and there is reasonable compliance with the routine. Due diligence does not require an individual acting for the organization to communicate information unless such communication is part of his regular duties or unless he has reason to know of the transaction and that the transaction would be materially affected by the information.

children.heading: Subrogation
children.label: 2 Subrogation
children.text: Where, under local law, one person is subrogated to the rights of another with respect to a lien or interest, such person shall be subrogated to such rights for purposes of any lien imposed by section 6321 or 6324.

children.heading: Forfeitures
children.label: 3 Forfeitures
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, a forfeiture under local law of property seized by a law enforcement agency of a State, county, or other local governmental subdivision shall relate back to the time of seizure, except that this paragraph shall not apply to the extent that under local law the holder of an intervening claim or interest would have priority over the interest of the State, county, or other local governmental subdivision in the property.

children.label: A
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year, determined by substituting “calendar year 1996” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Withdrawal of notice in certain circumstances
children.label: j Withdrawal of notice in certain circumstances
A
children.text: the filing of such notice was premature or otherwise not in accordance with administrative procedures of the Secretary,

children.label: B
children.text: the taxpayer has entered into an agreement under section 6159 to satisfy the tax liability for which the lien was imposed by means of installment payments, unless such agreement provides otherwise,

children.label: C
children.text: the withdrawal of such notice will facilitate the collection of the tax liability, or

children.label: D
children.text: with the consent of the taxpayer or the National Taxpayer Advocate, the withdrawal of such notice would be in the best interests of the taxpayer (as determined by the National Taxpayer Advocate) and the United States.

children.heading: Notice to credit agencies, etc.
children.label: 2 Notice to credit agencies, etc.
children.text: Upon written request by the taxpayer with respect to whom a notice of a lien was withdrawn under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall promptly make reasonable efforts to notify credit reporting agencies, and any financial institution or creditor whose name and address is specified in such request, of the withdrawal of such notice. Any such request shall be in such form as the Secretary may prescribe.
